query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "a stylometric application of large language models" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (d) as a foundational methodology and canonical reference for the "stylometric application" of LLMs, specifically addressing the sub-domain of fingerprinting and identity attribution which is the core mechanism of stylometry. It also meets criterion (f) by providing the canonical alternative-vocabulary cluster (fingerprinting, watermarking, provenance) for the user's query regarding stylometric analysis of LLM outputs.
